content.text: I'll help you check your Mittwald projects and list all available backups. Let me start by listing your projects.

input.prompt:
The user wants to see their Mittwald projects and all available backups with details. 

Use the following MCP tools in sequence:
1. First call: mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list_projects to list all projects
2. For each project returned, call: mcp__mittwald__mittwald_backup_list_backups with the project ID to get all backups for that project

Format the results clearly showing:
- Project name and ID
- For each project, list all backups with:
  - Backup ID
  - Creation date
  - Status
  - Size (if available)
  - Description/notes

Return the complete information in a clear, organized format.

content.text:
The user wants to see their Mittwald projects and all available backups with details. 

Use the following MCP tools in sequence:
1. First call: mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list_projects to list all projects
2. For each project returned, call: mcp__mittwald__mittwald_backup_list_backups with the project ID to get all backups for that project

Format the results clearly showing:
- Project name and ID
- For each project, list all backups with:
  - Backup ID
  - Creation date
  - Status
  - Size (if available)
  - Description/notes

Return the complete information in a clear, organized format.

tool_use_result:
Error: Exit code 1
error: unknown command 'call'

Error: Exit code 1
error: unknown command 'run'

content.content:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

tool_use_result.stdout:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

tool_use_result.prompt:
The user wants to see their Mittwald projects and all available backups with details. 

Use the following MCP tools in sequence:
1. First call: mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list_projects to list all projects
2. For each project returned, call: mcp__mittwald__mittwald_backup_list_backups with the project ID to get all backups for that project

Format the results clearly showing:
- Project name and ID
- For each project, list all backups with:
  - Backup ID
  - Creation date
  - Status
  - Size (if available)
  - Description/notes

Return the complete information in a clear, organized format.
